rule.name: ESXi Loghost Config Tampering
rule.description: This detection identifies changes to the syslog loghost configuration on an ESXi host, which may indicate an attempt to disrupt log forwarding and evade detection.
rule.query: `esxi_syslog` Message="*Set called with key*" AND Message IN ("*Syslog.global.logHost*","*Syslog.global.logdir*") | rex field=_raw "key '(?<key>[^']+)', value '\"(?<value>[^\"]+)\"'" | rex field=_raw "Z (?<dest>[\w\.]+)\s" | stats min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime count by dest key value | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | `esxi_loghost_config_tampering_filter`
false_positives: Limited false positives in most environments, however tune as needed
